meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Advanced Checklist)
content.seo_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to master your first SaaS build.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no SaaS knowledge' wall requires shifting from manual coding to AI-augmented engineering. By leveraging Git as your architecture source of truth and integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you transform complex SaaS deployment into a modular, version-controlled workflow. This checklist bridges the gap between student theory and professional production-grade delivery.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambition. You have the logic, the drive, and the vision, but the bridge between a local script and a production-ready SaaS application feels like a chasm of arcane deployment logs and database migrations. You aren't just missing code; you are missing the architectural intuition that only comes from shipping.

We are going to dismantle that barrier. By unifying version control with AI-native development tools, we turn the Git history into a blueprint for your success. This isn't just about writing functions; it's about engineering systems that scale while you learn, turning your first project into the cornerstone of your professional portfolio.

steps.title: Architecting the SaaS Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, generate a robust relational database schema that defines your SaaS entities. This ensures your Git repository starts with a strong foundation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core requirements for a task management SaaS. Generate a comprehensive Prisma schema for users, projects, and tasks with appropriate relations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude init --prompt 'Generate a Prisma schema based on the provided requirements.md'

steps.title: Initializing the Git Flow
steps.description: Establish a professional branching strategy to isolate AI-generated feature modules, ensuring your main branch remains stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a git branching strategy for a rapid SaaS development cycle, including naming conventions for features and bug fixes.

steps.title: Generating Secure Auth Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to implement secure authentication boilerplate, ensuring best practices for session management are baked in.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure NextAuth.js configuration for a SaaS platform, including email verification and protected API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement NextAuth.js with Prisma adapter for user authentication'

steps.title: Automated Unit Testing
steps.description: Integrate automated tests into your Git commit lifecycle to ensure that AI-suggested code changes do not introduce regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of Jest unit tests for the core SaaS business logic functions generated in the previous steps.

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline Integration
steps.description: Configure your Git repository to trigger automated deployments, effectively turning your repo into a CI/CD engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow file that runs tests and deploys the application to Vercel upon pushing to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a .github/workflows/deploy.yml file for Vercel integration'

pro_tips: Treat your Git commit messages as documentation for your future self; use the Conventional Commits specification to track AI-driven architectural changes.
Always review AI-generated code for security vulnerabilities; treat Claude Code as a junior developer, not a senior architect.
Use Git tags to mark 'Stable Version' milestones; this creates a clear, impressive visual history for recruiters to review in your GitHub profile.
Implement a 'dry-run' phase in your deployment scripts to catch environment variable mismatches before they hit production.
Keep your context window clean by modularizing your AI prompts; break large features into smaller, single-file Git commits.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, push your repository to GitHub, document your architecture in the README, and start reaching out to beta testers to gather real user feedback.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an architect of digital systems. With these tools and this workflow, your capacity to ship is limited only by your imagination. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
